name: away_setpoint_change_not_override
description: Setback re-application while away must not be misdetected as a manual override
notes: Before Issue #221 fix, when the coordinator applied setback_cool (79F) on a classification
cycle while away, the thermostat_changed listener would see the setpoint change and flag it
as a manual override (manual_override_active=True). This would suppress all future setback
applications until the user returned home.
The root cause: coordinator's _async_thermostat_changed lacked an 'expected-state' guard
to distinguish CA-issued setpoint changes from user-initiated ones.
Issue #474 (coordinator-level Tier A coverage): migrated to use_coordinator=true. A real
ClimateAdvisorCoordinator is now constructed over dispatching FakeHass/FakeScheduler fakes,
so CA's own climate.set_temperature service call reaches the real _async_thermostat_changed
listener via the same state-feedback -> async_set() -> dispatch path a genuine HA round-trip
would take. Both assertions (setback_applied, override_not_detected) are evaluated for real
against the real coordinator, not skipped or approximated. Proving-slice scenario for #474.
skip_startup_coalesce=true is required: a freshly built coordinator has its real 5-minute
post-restart override-detection suppression window active (_startup_coalesce_active=True),
same as production. Without this flag, _async_thermostat_changed early-returns before ever
reaching the override-detection guard, and this scenario passes VACUOUSLY (found via a real
revert test: with the flag absent, disabling the actual guard in coordinator.py did NOT make
this scenario fail -- confirming it wasn't exercising the guard at all). With the flag present,
a revert test (temporarily stripping the guard conditions in _async_thermostat_changed's
elif branch, then restoring) confirmed override_detected correctly fires when the guard is
removed, and correctly does not fire with the guard intact -- this scenario is load-bearing.
Awaiting user review before promotion to golden/ per the Golden Simulation Test Policy.

events.note: System is in away mode from the start of this scenario
Hot day classification â€” cool mode; away setback (79F) should be applied and must NOT trigger override detection
Steady state check: system in away mode 30 min after setback was applied

assertions.reason: Classification while away applies setback_cool (79F) via the real coordinator + engine (Issue #474) — this is the CA-issued setpoint change that must NOT be detected as an override.

The coordinator's real _async_thermostat_changed must NOT set manual_override_active=True when CA itself issued the setback setpoint change. Evaluated for real (Issue #474 — coordinator constructed over dispatching fakes; CA's own set_temperature call reaches the real listener via async_set() dispatch, same as a genuine HA round-trip).

verdict.summary: CA-issued setback setpoint change while away is not flagged as a manual override
verdict.observed_behavior: Both setback_applied (79F) and override_not_detected pass against the real coordinator's _async_thermostat_changed listener (Issue #474).
verdict.expected_behavior: setback_applied (79F) at classification event; manual_override_active remains False after CA-issued setpoint change
